content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

December 31, 1993

Helen Williams
720-695 Alexander Lane
Standish, CA 96128

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-93-457

Dear Ms. Williams:

You have requested advice regarding the campaign disclosure
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1

QUESTIONS

1. Will the Political Reform Act require you to discontinue
your radio talk show during your campaign for the lassen County
Board of Supervisors, and if so, for what period of time?

2. If elected will you be able to serve on the Lassen
County Board of Supervisors and continue to host your talk show?

3. May you do spot commercials on the radio station on
behalf of your candidacy at times other than during the talk show?

4. Can the air time available to callers during your talk
show be construed as "equal time" provided for an opponent?

5. If you stay on the air during the campaign, will the
value of that air time be considered an "in-kind" contribution
from the radio station?

CONCLUSIONS

1-4. There are no provisions in the Act which would require
you to cease doing your talk show during your campaign,
prevent you from serving on the Lassen County Board of Supervisors
while you continue to host your talk show, or prevent you from
doing spot commercials for your candidacy on the radio. In

1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Page 2
Williams, Helen
A-93-457


addition, the Act does not regulate the issue of "equal time"
regarding the broadcasts of federally regulated broadcast outlets.
You may want to contact the Federal Communications Commission
(FCC) regarding this issue.

5.     The facts you present indicate that the radio station is
not motivated by political purposes in employing you to do this
program. Therefore, conducting your talk show while you are
campaigning for a public office would not result in an in-kind
contribution to you by the radio station which employs you.

FACTS

     You are the hostess of a radio talk show on KSUE radio in
Susanville, California. You indicated in our telephone
conversation of December 7, 1993, that KSUE is licensed by the FCC
to operate a radio station. Your program runs approximately 45
minutes, five days per week, with an open line discussion on any
subject callers wish to discuss.

     You are contemplating running for a seat on the Lassen County
Board of Supervisors, District 5, in June of 1994.

ANALYSIS

     With regard to your question whether the radio station would
be making an in-kind contribution to your campaign, Government
Code Section 82015 defines "contribution" as:

     ...a payment, a forgiveness of a loan, a payment of a loan by
a third party, or an enforceable promise to make a payment
except to the extent that full and adequate consideration is
received unless it is clear from the surrounding
circumstances that it is not made for political purposes. An
expenditure made at the behest of a candidate, committee of
elected officer is a contribution to the candidate, committee
or elected officer unless full and adequate consideration is
received for making the expenditure.

                                        (Emphasis added.)

     Thus, in order to be construed as a contribution, a payment
must be made for political purposes. (Neal Advice Letter, No. A-
93-276.) Regulation 18215 provides that a payment is made for
political purposes if it is for the purpose of influencing or
attempting to influence the action of the voters for or against
the nomination or election of any candidate or if the payment was
received by or made at the behest of the candidate, committee or
an organization formed primarily for political purposes.

     There are no facts to indicate that KSUE will be making a
contribution for political purposes. Furthermore, the
continuation of your employment as a talk show hostess under these

Page 3
Williams, Helen
A-93-457


circumstances during your candidacy will not constitute a
contribution nor an expenditure made on your behalf.

If you have additional questions, please contact me at (916)
322-5662.

Sincerely,

Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel
[signature]
By: Wayne P. Imberi
Political Reform Consultant
